meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve Stuck App Syndrome
content.seo_description: Break through the beginner's plateau. Learn to implement professional user onboarding using Antigravity and Claude Code to transform your portfolio projects.
content.answer_block: Most student projects fail because users drop off immediately. To solve this, implement a state-driven onboarding flow. By using Antigravity for UI orchestration and Claude Code for rapid feature iteration, you can build a high-retention experience that proves your technical maturity and makes your portfolio stand out to recruiters.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks coding, but your app feels empty the moment a user hits the dashboard. That 'blank slate' anxiety is the silent killer of every student project. Users don't know where to click, the value proposition is lost, and your hard work sits gathering digital dust.

It’s time to move beyond simple CRUD applications. By mastering user onboarding, you shift from being a 'coder' to a 'product engineer.' This transition is exactly what separates a hobbyist project from a professional-grade SaaS application. Let's build a flow that guides your users to their 'Aha!' moment.

steps.title: Mapping the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Define the single action that delivers value to the user. Onboarding should exist only to get them to this moment as fast as possible.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current app functionality and identify the core value proposition. Create a 3-step user onboarding flow that guides a new user to the primary feature while minimizing friction.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Analyze current codebase and suggest a 3-step onboarding flow JSON schema'

steps.title: State Management Setup
steps.description: Ensure your app remembers if a user has completed the onboarding. We will use local storage or a database flag to toggle the UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React-based state management hook for tracking onboarding completion status and conditionally rendering the onboarding modal.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/hooks/useOnboarding.ts --action 'Implement state persistence for onboarding status'

steps.title: Designing the Guided Tour
steps.description: Build the visual components that highlight key features. Keep text minimal and action-oriented.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive, accessible onboarding modal component using Tailwind CSS that highlights the main navigation area.

steps.title: Progressive Disclosure
steps.description: Don't overwhelm the user. Reveal advanced features only after the initial onboarding is complete.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my dashboard components to hide advanced settings until the `hasCompletedOnboarding` state is true.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/components/Dashboard.tsx --action 'Wrap advanced features in a conditional guard'

steps.title: Telemetry and Feedback
steps.description: Add a simple event tracker to see where users drop off during your onboarding flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add basic console logging or a simple event tracking utility to monitor when a user completes each step of the onboarding flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/utils/analytics.ts --action 'Add onboarding progress events'

pro_tips: Never ask for user permission or data before providing value; always let them see the dashboard first.
Use 'empty states' as onboarding—instead of showing nothing, show a 'Get Started' button inside the empty dashboard.
Keep your onboarding under 3 screens; if it takes longer, your product is likely too complex for a MVP.
Add a 'Skip' button. Forcing users through a tutorial creates resentment, not engagement.
Use confetti or a subtle micro-interaction upon completion to reward the user for finishing the setup.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding is live, deploy your project to Vercel or Netlify and share the link in our community Slack channel for a code review.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build professional, high-retention applications that recruiters actually notice. Don't stop here; iterate on your UI and keep pushing the boundaries of your technical skills. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
